Hello, I am Maria Clara. I lead the delegation from my university.
It is a pleasure to meet you. How is the conference for you so far?
In my opinion, we should start with the main topic.
Sorry, would you mind repeating that?
No problem. I will handle the introduction.
I speak English, and I am improving.
Hello. I do not think we have met. I am Daniel, from a university in Portugal.
Nice to meet you, Daniel. I am Maria Clara. I lead the delegation from my university in Brazil.
Brazil. Welcome to Seoul. How was your trip?
It was long, but it was fine. While we were waiting for the flight, we reviewed the whole agenda.
That is good planning. What is your priority for the conference?
My priority is to present our work clearly, and to meet colleagues like you. What about you?
The same. By the way, do you know when the first round table starts?
Yes. The agenda says ten o'clock, in the main hall. Would you like to sit together?

Hello, this is a message to myself, a small progress check before Seoul. Fifteen lessons ago, I was afraid to say one word in English. Today I can introduce myself, I can talk about my role at the university, and I can tell a story about a past event. I still make mistakes with the tenses, and sometimes I need a moment to find the word. That is fine. Last week I spoke for three minutes without switching to Portuguese, and that was a first for me. My priority now is to keep practicing every day and to feel calm when I speak. I am going to be ready for the conference. I speak English, and I am improving.

Good morning, and welcome to the International University Leadership Conference here in Seoul. Here is a short announcement about today's agenda for the visiting delegation. The welcome reception starts at nine in the main hall, so please arrive a few minutes early. At ten, we begin the first round table, where each institution presents its work and shares an opinion on the main topic. If you do not understand a question, do not worry; you can always ask the speaker to repeat it. There is one small change of plan: the interpreter will not be here this morning, so the introductions will be in English. In the evening, there is a dinner for the whole delegation at seven. We recommend that you rest a little before it. Thank you, and enjoy the conference.
